The prophet is to judge the violent city; the city of violence. Jerusalem is called this because of her crimes. The sins that Jerusalem is accused of are extremely sinful. Murder, idolatry, disobedience to parents, oppression and extortion, profanation of the Sabbath and holy things, violations of the seventh commandment, lewdness and adultery. A lack of awareness of God was at the root of all this wickedness. Sinners anger God because they forget him. Jerusalem has filled up her measure of sins. Those who give themselves over to be ruled by their desires will justly be given over to be controlled by them. Those who decide to be their own masters should expect no happiness other than what their own hands can provide; and it will prove to be a miserable portion.

Israel, when compared with other nations, had been like gold and silver compared with cheaper metals. But they were now like waste that is burned in the furnace or thrown away when silver is refined. Sinners, especially those who have turned away from their faith, are, in God’s view, useless and good for nothing. When God brings his own people into hardship, he sits by them as a refiner does with gold, to see that they are not kept there any longer than is fitting and necessary. The impurities will be completely removed, and the valuable metal purified. Let those who suffer pain or prolonged sickness, and find that their hearts can barely endure these minor and temporary troubles, take warning to flee from God’s anger; for if these trials are not made helpful by the power of the Holy Spirit, to cleanse their hearts and hands from sin, far worse things will come upon them.

All levels of people had helped to fill up the measure of the nation's guilt. Those who held any authority abused it, and even merchants found ways to oppress one another. It is a bad sign for a people when judgments are falling on them, and prayer is restricted. Let all who fear God unite to promote his truth and righteousness; as wicked people of every rank and profession plot together to harm them.
